reference.name: The Court Service
reference.description: (Blank) Letter heading, appears above address lines

reference.name: The Court Service Minicom
reference.description: (Blank) Letter heading, appears under the address lines

reference.name: Your Reference
reference.description: Part of a Letter heading, stating the addressee's reference

reference.name: Our Reference
reference.description: Part of a Letter heading, stating the court service reference

reference.name: Salutation
reference.description: Part of a Letter heading, the salutation before the name of the addressee

reference.name: Case Number
reference.description: Part of a Letter heading, the case number field label

reference.name: Regarding
reference.description: Part of a Letter heading, the regarding label

reference.name: Versus
reference.description: Part of a Letter heading, party versus party

reference.name: Extention
reference.description: Part of a Letter signature by CS staff, which typically includes Extention

reference.name: Section
reference.description: Part of a Letter signature by CS staff, which typically includes Section

reference.name: Closing
reference.description: Part of a Letter signature by CS staff, the closeing (as opposed to Salutation)

reference.name: DX
reference.description: Part of a Letter heading, part of the court address

reference.name: Fax
reference.description: Part of a Letter heading, part of the court address

reference.name: Telephone
reference.description: Part of a Letter heading, part of the court address

reference.value: The court office at is open between 10am and 4pm Monday to Friday.
The court sent it to the defendant by first class post on and it will be deemed served on .
The defendant has until to reply.
_Pay an amount into court to satisfy your claim. This is called 'payment in satisfaction'. The court will notify you of the payment and you will have to decide whether to accept the amount offered.
_Offer to pay you an amount to satisfy your claim and/or ask for time to pay. The court will send you a copy of the defendant's reply and you will have to decide what you want to do.

_Admit liability for your claim but not offer an amount in satisfaction. The court will send you a copy of the defendant's reply and you will be able to request judgment for amount to be decided by the court and costs.

_File an acknowledgement of service. This will allow the defendant 28 days from the date of service of your particulars of claim to file a defence or contest the court's jurisdiction. The court will send you a notice that an acknowledgement has been filed.
_Dispute your claim. The court will send you a copy of the defence and tell you what to do next.

_Not reply at all. You may ask the court to enter judgment for an amount to be decided by the court by completing the tear off portion on this form. A hearing will be arranged to determine the amount the defendant should pay you.If you do not request judgment within 6 months of the date for filing a defence, your claim will be stayed. This means that the only action open to you would be to apply to a judge for an order lifting the stay.
_The court will notify both you and the defendant of any steps you should take to prepare for the hearing at which the court will decide what amount you are entitled to.
_You should notify the court immediately if your claim is settled or you decide that you do not wish to proceed.
The defendant has not filed an admission or defence to my claim, or an application to contest the court's jurisdiction and the time for doing so has expired.
I request that judgment be entered for an amount including costs, to be decided by the court.
